mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5. It provides a simple interface for deploying and interacting with these models in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the complexity of managing model versions and resource requirements.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface (CLI) on your local machine, which is necessary for interacting with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster, allowing you to interact with it using kubectl.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with gcloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Ollama application to your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/charts

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.6 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Install Ollama in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Ollama deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment ollama --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace ollama

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources allocated. Monitor your usage to avoid unexpected charges.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama from Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the Ollama deployment.
> This will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama to a New Version
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will drain and cordon nodes if necessary, upgrade the Ollama deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.6 --namespace ollama

kubectl get pods --namespace ollama

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --region=us-central1' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Kubernetes cannot pull the Ollama image from the repository.

**Fix:**
Check your image repository settings and ensure the image exists: 'kubectl describe pod <pod-name>' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the Ollama pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size of your nodes: 'gcloud container clusters resize ollama-cluster --size=<new-size>'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Ollama application is crashing repeatedly.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod: 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace ollama' for more information.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including Kimi-K2.5 and GLM-5, improved REST API endpoints.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace ollama' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
